title: Migrate procbomber generator from bench to Kaboom Crew — replace player.1 + bot.1 prefab meshes
intent: Once the multi-mesh tree (GDP-001) + parameter expansion (GDP-002) + palette (GDP-003) + texturing (GDP-004) + accessories (GDP-005) stabilise in the procbomber-bench sandbox, migrate the whole generator stack into the production Kaboom Crew project. Replace the current static `player.prefab.json` + `bot.prefab.json` meshes with procedural mesh trees. Recipe sources: `?recipe=<base64>` for player.1, `?botRecipe=<base64>` for bot.1, family-seeded defaults otherwise. ECS gameplay (BomberStats, GridMover, GridPosition, blast collision, round resolve) is unchanged — only the visual shell swaps. The bench stays as the iteration sandbox for further generator changes.
rationale: Source: the user's headline goal — 'modern network game' — depends on visibly distinct bombers, especially in multiplayer where multiple humans must read each other in 200ms. The current capsule + sphere prefabs do not deliver that. The bench has been the right place to iterate; migration is the moment the gameplay finally benefits. Sequenced last in the GDP-2026-05-21 batch because everything else feeds into it: by the time this lands, the recipe + palette + accessories + texturing are stable enough to be 'this is what a Kaboom Crew bomber looks like'.
acceptanceHints: Depends on GDP-2026-05-21-001 + GDP-2026-05-21-003 minimum. GDP-002 / 004 / 005 / 006 enhance but don't block.

Move (don't copy) the generators from examples/procbomber-bench/src/generators/ to examples/kaboom-crew/src/characters/. The bench keeps importing from the new location — the bench's job is testing the generator, so co-location with the game makes sense.

Update examples/kaboom-crew/bootstrap.ts: register the procedural-mesh builder with the engine's procedural mesh registry (same registry from S101 AGF-PROCMESH-REGISTRY). The mesh key for Kaboom Crew is `procedural:kaboom-bomber` (distinct from `procedural:procbomber` so the bench can keep its own copy if it diverges).

Update examples/kaboom-crew/prefabs/player.prefab.json + bot.prefab.json: MeshRenderer.mesh = 'procedural:kaboom-bomber'. Transform / GridPosition / BomberStats / GridMover / minimap-marker components stay exactly as they are.
Recipe sources, in priority order (this is the per-bomber recipe lookup at spawn time):
1. `?recipe=<base64-json>` URL parameter applied to player.1 (DEBUG / AGENT / sharing surface — kept as debug-only per characters-and-visual.md §6.2).
2. `?botRecipe=<base64-json>` URL parameter applied to bot.1.
3. Per-entity recipe stored on the entity (post-multiplayer: the server hands out recipes when a client connects).
4. Fallback default recipes: player.1 uses seed=1 (first family in the palette table — `sky`); bot.1 uses seed=2 (`ember`). Two visibly distinct bombers with no setup required.

Agent surface: `window.__agf.kaboom.spawnWithRecipe(entityId, recipe)` — accepts a CharacterRecipe, validates it, applies it to the entity's MeshRenderer by re-acquiring the procedural mesh handle. Returns Promise<void> resolving when the swap completes. Mirrors the bench's adapter.setMeshGeometry path; uses the entity ECS layer for the in-flight swap.

HUD impact: minimap marker colour pulls from recipe.palette.head (or the family primary). This is the one place the recipe is player-visible in MVP. Bottom-left HUD per-bomber line stays text-only — no recipe info displayed.

Gameplay invariant: every existing Kaboom Crew test (unit + bot-vs-bot integration + Playwright smoke) PASSES UNCHANGED after migration. The mesh tree is a presentation swap; rules + stats + collision are identical.

Death animation interaction: the existing kaboom death tween (S90 KABOOM-DEATH-FALL + GDP-004 slapstick refinement) operates on the entity's root Transform. Post-migration, it still does — root.rotation + root.position drives the whole bomber. The limb-pivot rotations from GDP-009 layer on top of root-Transform animation, not replace it. Document this composition order in the bootstrap comments.

Multiplayer interaction (forward-looking, not blocking this story): when GDP-2026-05-20-007 (network profile) lands, the SERVER becomes the source of truth for each player's recipe. Recipe is part of the joinWorld payload; client renders what the server tells it. URL recipe overrides remain client-side for solo + debug only.

Cleanup: when a bomber entity despawns (round restart, leave-world), its procedural mesh handle is released via the renderer's existing dispose path. Verify no Three.js objects leak across rounds (existing leak-hunt recipe from docs/agent-debug-recipes.md applies).

Recording compatibility: the deterministic recording fixture (recordings/demo-30s.recording.json) is rendered with the post-migration mesh. The recording's gameplay events are unchanged — only the visual replay looks different. Regenerate the recording in this PR.

Bench retention: examples/procbomber-bench/ stays as a separate AGF project. Its bootstrap continues to register `procedural:procbomber` (its own key), pointing at the SAME generator module under examples/kaboom-crew/src/characters/. The bench is the dev iteration sandbox; the game is the production consumer. No copy-paste between them.

Acceptance smoke: `npm run dev` → /?project=kaboom-crew → player.1 and bot.1 visible as procedural bombers, visibly distinct, with default family + accessory seeds. Difficulty preset still applies. Round resolve still works. Auto-restart still works.
Acceptance debug: `?recipe=<base64>` URL → player.1 renders the specified recipe; reload with same URL produces identical mesh.
Acceptance agent: `__agf.kaboom.spawnWithRecipe('bot.1', { seed: 99 })` mid-round → bot.1's visuals swap, gameplay state preserved, no crash.

Out of scope (explicit): server-side recipe sync (waits for the network story), persistent profile (waits for cosmetic-unlock infrastructure), animation refresh tied to limb pivots (waits for GDP-2026-05-20-009 the full anim pack). This story is a visual swap; the production-quality animation experience lands when GDP-009 finishes.
